'''Christopher Morley''' (born 22 September 1973) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s.<ref name="Morley Lied To Play For Wales"/> He played as a {{rlp|pr|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} at club level for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], [[Sheffield Eagles]], [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref>Graham Williams, Peter Lush, David Farrar (November 2009). "The British Rugby League Records Book [Page-108…114]". London League Publications Ltd. {{ISBN|978-1-903659-49-6}}</ref>

==Background==
Morley was born in [[County Borough of Salford|Salford]], [[Lancashire]], England.

==Playing career==
===International honours===
Chris Morley won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] while at St. Helens in 1996 against France ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), and England, while at Salford in 1999 against Ireland and Scotland, while at Sheffield Eagles in 2000 against South Africa ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), while at Leigh in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] against Lebanon ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]), New Zealand, Papua New Guinea ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]) and Australia, while at Oldham in 2001 against England, while at Halifax in 2003 against Russia, and Australia, while at Swinton in 2006 against Scotland, 1996...2006 13(14?)-caps + 4-caps ([[Rugby league positions#Interchange|interchange]]/[[Substitution (sport)|substitute]]) 1(2?)-try 4(8?)-points.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

It was later revealed, in his brother, [[Adrian Morley]]'s autobiography ''"Moz"'' that Chris Morley did not actually qualify to represent Wales, he had lied to the then Wales Assistant Coach; [[Mike Gregory]], and that Morley's grandmother was born in a village just outside [[Swansea]] when in fact she was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]], [[Lancashire]], and therefore he had no Welsh qualification.<ref name="Morley Lied To Play For Wales">{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2012/11/13/morley-lied-to-play-for-wales|title=Morley Lied To Play For Wales|publisher=nrl.com|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref>

===Challenge Cup Final appearances===
Chris Morley was a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 32–22 victory over [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[1997 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League II]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 3 May 1997.

==Genealogical information==
Chris Morley is the older brother of the rugby league footballer; [[Adrian Morley]].
